summary: DHH recounts his rapid conversion from PC/Windows XP to Apple/OSX after just 24 days. Despite acknowledging a speed loss compared to his Windows machine, he finds OSX's design, polish, and overall experience more than compensate. He's so convinced he's ready to sell his PC desktop and replace it with a 17-inch iMac.
key_points: Complete platform conversion from Windows XP to Mac OSX in under a month
Design quality and user experience outweigh raw performance advantages
Frames Microsoft as a downward spiral that Apple offers a genuine alternative to
Early signal of DHH's lasting preference for Apple's aesthetic-first philosophy
money_quotes: 24 days. That's how long it took for me to be totally swept away by the Apple offering.
I'm ready to cut my PC connection and retreat into the snow covered domain of hardware running OSX.
It's smarter, slicker, and smoother. It's aesthetic. Pure.
A more than viable, a capable, alternative to the spiral of Microsoft.
key_insight: Superior design and user experience can justify sacrificing raw performance — a philosophy that would come to define DHH's approach to tools and technology.
